reasoning: Applied the error taxonomy (grammar, vocabulary, pragmatic categories) with severity calibrated against B2 CEFR descriptors. At B2, articles should be mostly acquired for concrete nouns but abstract-noun article usage is at the acquisition boundary. Preposition collocations transfer from Ukrainian L1 (which uses different prepositions or none). Conditional structures are a known B2 developmental challenge compounded by Ukrainian L1 transfer of "yakscho b" = "if would". Interlanguage analysis identified systematic L1 transfer patterns from Ukrainian (zero-article L1, different preposition government, present tense for durations).

errors.corrected: it was a completely different area
errors.explanation: Missing indefinite article before singular countable noun. At B2, article use with concrete nouns should be acquired.

errors.original: the privacy means that I control...
errors.explanation: Overuse of definite article with abstract noun used as general concept. Recurring pattern throughout lesson.

errors.original: I think the capitalism always finds new ways
errors.corrected: I think capitalism always finds new ways
errors.explanation: Overuse of definite article with abstract noun "capitalism" as general concept. Same pattern as #2.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: Same Ukrainian zero-article L1 transfer with hypercorrection. Andrii acknowledged the pattern at 09:23: "I know this but I automatically add 'the'."

errors.explanation: Overuse of definite article with modified abstract noun. Even with an adjective, abstract nouns as general concepts take zero article.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: Same pattern as #2 and #3. Andrii noted the adjective did not change the rule.

errors.explanation: Missing indefinite article before profession. Singular countable nouns require an article.
Wrong preposition with "depend". Ukrainian "zalezhyty vid" uses "vid" (from), transferred to English.

errors.original: companies are interested about collecting more data
errors.corrected: companies are interested in collecting more data
errors.explanation: Wrong preposition with "interested". Ukrainian uses a different preposition for this collocation.
Added unnecessary preposition after transitive verb "discuss". English "discuss" is transitive; no preposition needed.

errors.original: if you would tell people to pay five dollars
errors.corrected: if you told people to pay five dollars
errors.explanation: Used "would" in the if-clause of a second conditional. English second conditional requires past simple in the if-clause.
Used "will" in the if-clause of a first conditional. Also overused "the" with generic "government".
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: Same conditional transfer pattern. Ukrainian future in if-clauses is grammatical; English prohibits "will" in if-clauses.

errors.original: manipulate the democracy" and "threat to the society
errors.explanation: Two instances of definite article overuse with abstract nouns in the same utterance.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: Same systematic pattern as #2-#4. Andrii noted at 13:34: "When thinking about ideas, the articles just come out wrong."

errors.original: the freedom of speech and the privacy are fundamental rights
errors.corrected: freedom of speech and privacy are fundamental rights
errors.explanation: Overuse of "the" with abstract nouns used as general concepts. Occurred during passionate speech.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: Same pattern. Andrii noted: "When I get emotional all grammar monitoring turns off."

errors.original: I am working in tech for five years
errors.corrected: I have been working in tech for five years
errors.explanation: Used present continuous instead of present perfect continuous for a duration from past to present.
